sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Perimeter Announces Date for First Quarter 2026 Earnings Call
feed.summary: Perimeter Solutions (NYSE: PRM) announced it will release its financial results for the first quarter 2026 on Wednesday, May 6, 2026, before the market opens. The company will host a conference call at 8:30 a.m. ET on the same day to discuss these results, led by CEO Haitham Khouri, CFO Kyle Sable, and Head of Investor Relations Seth Barker. The webcast will be accessible through Perimeter’s investor relations website and a replay will be available until June 6, 2026.

feed.title: SC business owners sue Dave & Busters over gambling laws
feed.summary: A group of South Carolina business owners has filed a lawsuit against Dave & Busters, alleging that the arcade machines in their Greenville and Myrtle Beach locations violate state gambling laws. The lawsuit, filed by SC Citizens For Equal Enforcement of Gambling Laws, LLC, seeks to highlight what they claim is selective enforcement of gambling laws against local businesses and asks for refunds for customers who lost money playing arcade games. The group's counsel states their goal is to bring clarity and equality to the video game entertainment industry in South Carolina.

feed.title: Plexus Corp Stock Gains 91 Percent Over One Year
feed.summary: Plexus Corp. (NASDAQ:PLXS) was a top contributor to the Riverwater Sustainable Value Strategy in Q1 2026, with its stock gaining 91.71 percent over the past year. The electronics manufacturer reported strong fiscal Q2 results, driven by its Industrial and Healthcare segments, and is noted for leveraging AI for process improvement. Riverwater Partners expects a market shift towards fundamental valuation metrics, which should benefit high-quality small-cap companies like Plexus Corp.

feed.title: Wex COO Joel Dearborn Jr sells $612,500 in company stock
feed.summary: WEX Inc.'s COO, Joel Dearborn Jr, sold 3,500 shares of company stock for $612,500 on April 16, 2026, as the stock approached its 52-week high. The sale was pre-planned, and Dearborn still retains a significant number of shares directly and indirectly. Despite the sale, InvestingPro analysis suggests WEX is undervalued, and the company recently surpassed Q4 2025 earnings expectations.

feed.title: American Express Hypercard Deal Deepens AI Role In Corporate Spending
feed.summary: American Express (NYSE:AXP) is acquiring Hypercard, an AI-focused expense management startup, to enhance AI-driven automation for commercial expense workflows within its corporate offerings. This acquisition aims to deepen American Express's role in the day-to-day finance operations for businesses, making it more competitive against other corporate card and software providers. The deal reinforces Amex's strategy of using technology and AI to boost engagement with commercial and premium customers, though integration risks and competitive pressures exist.

feed.title: RBB Bancorp (RBB) Earnings Rebound And 28.1% Margin Test Bearish Profitability Narrative
feed.summary: RBB Bancorp (RBB) reported a strong Q1 2026 with a rebound in earnings and a 28.1% net profit margin, challenging a five-year earnings decline trend. The company's recent performance shows a 19.8% earnings growth, improved credit quality, and better efficiency, attracting bullish investors. However, skeptics point to the longer-term earnings decline and an analyst price target below the current share price, urging caution.

feed.title: Legislation to ban pharmacies from owning PBMs heads to Gov. Lee's desk, CVS Health may close Tenn. locations
feed.summary: The Tennessee House passed a bill, the Fair RX Act, that would ban pharmacies from owning Pharmacy Benefits Managers (PBMs), sending it to Gov. Bill Lee's desk after it also passed in the Senate. CVS Health, which owns the PBM Caremark, has warned that if the bill becomes law, it will lead to the closure of all its Tennessee locations, including 25 MinuteClinic locations, and the loss of over 2,000 jobs, arguing the legislation will not lower drug costs and is solely designed to benefit independent pharmacies. Critics anticipate increased prescription costs and legal challenges, noting a similar ban in Arkansas was blocked by a federal court.

feed.title: Ex-patient grateful for funding as Jim Pattison Children's Hospital gets $2M from Mosaic
feed.summary: The Mosaic Company has donated $2 million to the Jim Pattison Children's Hospital Foundation, continuing its long-term partnership. This funding will support the hospital's Child Life program, which provides activities and support to help children cope with treatment, as well as the Child Life Zone. An 18-year-old cancer survivor, Abir Raiyan, expressed profound gratitude for the program's positive impact during his three years of treatment at the hospital.

feed.title: Irving-Based McKesson Sells $1.25B Stake in Medical-Surgical Unit Ahead of Planned IPO
feed.summary: McKesson Corporation has sold a minority stake in its Medical-Surgical Solutions (MMS) business to Apollo Funds for $1.25 billion, valuing the unit at $13 billion. This transaction is a strategic step as McKesson prepares to spin off MMS into an independent, publicly traded company, with an IPO planned for the second half of 2027. McKesson will maintain operating control and majority ownership of MMS, emphasizing the move to unlock shareholder value and focus on high-growth opportunities within its core business.

feed.title: WR Berkley Q1 2026 Earnings Call: Complete Transcript
feed.summary: WR Berkley (NYSE:WRB) reported strong Q1 2026 results with a record net investment income of $404 million and solid underwriting profits, achieving a return on beginning equity of 21.2%. Net income reached $515 million, or $1.31 per share, with a calendar year combined ratio of 90.7%. Despite increased competition, particularly in reinsurance and property markets, the company plans to focus on growth in attractive casualty lines while maintaining disciplined underwriting and expects to return significant capital to shareholders.

feed.title: Elon Musk Agrees GTA 6 Could Be Made 'In Minutes' With AI — Take‑Two CEO Fires Back: 'Wouldn't It Take His Job?'
feed.summary: Billionaire Elon Musk suggested that AI could create a game like GTA 6 "in minutes," a claim that Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick countered by questioning its impact on Musk's own work. Zelnick argued that if AI could eliminate jobs, it would logically target the "richest man on Earth" first, highlighting Musk's busy schedule despite his use of AI. This exchange follows earlier remarks from Zelnick dismissing Musk's previous comments on AI's ability to create video game "hits."

feed.title: VGIT vs FIGB: Comparing Intermediate Treasury and Investment Grade Bond ETFs
feed.summary: This article compares two bond ETFs, the Vanguard Intermediate-Term Treasury ETF (VGIT) and the Fidelity Investment Grade Bond ETF (FIGB), highlighting their differing strategies, costs, and risk profiles. VGIT offers a lower-cost, government-only approach with U.S. Treasuries, while FIGB provides broader corporate exposure with a higher yield but also a higher expense ratio and increased credit risk. The choice between them depends on an investor's individual goals and risk tolerance for income and portfolio stabilization.

feed.title: Consumer Federation of America Class Action Complaint & Jury Trial Demand Against Meta Platforms, Inc
feed.summary: The Consumer Federation of America (CFA) has filed a class action complaint and jury trial demand against Meta Platforms, Inc. in the Superior Court of the District of Columbia. CFA alleges that Meta misleads D.C. consumers about its efforts to combat fraud, claiming the company profits from scam advertisements that proliferate on its platforms. The lawsuit, filed on behalf of D.C. Facebook users, seeks damages, illegal profits, and injunctive relief under the D.C. Consumer Protection Procedures Act.

feed.title: Stellar Bancorp Sets May Vote on $2 Billion Merger With Prosperity Bancshares
feed.summary: Stellar Bancorp will hold a special shareholder meeting on May 27, 2026, to vote on its proposed $2 billion merger with Prosperity Bancshares. feed.title: Oilfield Service Firm Halliburton is Discussing Commercial Terms With Customers for Venezuela Operations
feed.summary: Halliburton is actively discussing commercial terms with customers for restarting operations in Venezuela, driven by renewed interest in the country's oil reserves following the easing of U.S. sanctions. CEO Jeff Miller confirmed discussions are progressing and many of the company's facilities are in better condition than expected. Halliburton, which exited Venezuela in 2020, has also begun recruiting engineers and technicians for its potential return.

feed.title: Fifth Third shareholders re-elect board, approve auditor

The company also announced preliminary results from its Annual Shareholders Meeting, which included an advisory approval of executive compensation. Fifth Third Bancorp recently reported strong first-quarter 2026 earnings, surpassing EPS expectations, and closed its acquisition of Comerica, with analysts reiterating a Buy rating on the stock.
